slice: lotus-core TransactionLedgerWindow realized outcome source adapter
implemented: Source-owned trade_fee transaction-row scalar wraps into COST outcome evidence.
Source-owned withholding_tax_amount transaction-row scalar wraps into TAX outcome evidence.
Source-owned realized_fx_pnl transaction-row scalar wraps into FX_RESIDUAL outcome evidence.
Source-owned linked cashflow.amount transaction-row scalar wraps into CASH_RESIDUAL outcome evidence.

Adapter preserves lotus-core product identity, portfolio id, as-of date, generated/evidence timestamp, data-quality posture, source fingerprint, transaction id, transaction type, selected measure, selected source field, source units, reason codes, and content hash.
Adapter blocks missing transaction rows and missing selected source values instead of fabricating evidence.
Adapter carries degraded source-owner data-quality posture into the realized source snapshot.
explicitly_not_claimed: No execution quality, fill quality, liquidity ladder, or rule-outcome inference.

focused_validation.result: passed: lint, format, monetary-float guard, no-alias guard, mypy, OpenAPI quality, API vocabulary, domain-data-product, trust-telemetry, observability, and 928 unit tests

critical_review.completeness: Complete for explicit TransactionLedgerWindow row-level scalar evidence currently exposed by source-owned lotus-core contracts.
critical_review.correctness: The adapter selects only explicit source fields and maps them to RFC-0042 dimensions without recomputation.
critical_review.testing_quality: Focused tests exercise ready evidence, degraded source posture, ready TAX assembly, missing transaction row blocking, and missing selected value blocking.
critical_review.code_quality: Implementation reuses existing core source snapshot helpers and keeps transaction-specific selection logic isolated in small helpers.
critical_review.production_readiness: Full repository gate passed locally. Ready for wiki synchronization check, PR checks, merge, wiki publication, and post-merge validation.
